title: Shallow structure of the Northern Chilean marine forearc between 19°S–21°S using multichannel seismic reflection and refraction data
journal: Journal of South American Earth Sciences
abstract: The research combines high-resolution bathymetry and seismic data to examine the northern Chilean erosive convergent margin between 19°S and 21°S. The study reveals a regionally developed forearc basin containing previously recognized features, with sediment accumulation reaching approximately 2 km at its deepest point along the middle slope. The investigation proposes that subsidence from subduction erosion mechanisms explains the basin formation process. Four trench-perpendicular marine seismic profiles from the MGL1610 (PICTURES) cruise are analyzed using multichannel seismic reflection and refraction methods.

location.description: Offshore northern Chile between 19°S and 21°S, Iquique region forearc

acquisition.vessel: R/V Marcus G. Langseth (MGL1610 — PICTURES cruise)

data.name: MGL1610 Multichannel Seismic Reflection Data (PICTURES cruise)
data.description: MCS reflection shot data (SEGD format) acquired during the 2016 PICTURES experiment offshore northern Chile (19°–21°S). Four trench-perpendicular profiles analyzed for forearc structure. Raw shot data archived at MGDS dataset 23934.

data.name: MGL1610 OBS Wide-angle Refraction Data (XW Network, IRIS/EarthScope)
data.description: 51 OBS stations (XW network, 2016) deployed during MGL1610 cruise offshore northern Chile. Wide-angle seismic refraction data accessible via IRIS/EarthScope web services.

data.name: MGL1610 Bathymetry:Swath (Multibeam EM122) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw multibeam swath bathymetry, backscatter, and sidescan data (2,642 files, ~170 GB) acquired with Kongsberg EM122 during the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise offshore northern Chile 19°–21°S.

data.name: MGL1610 Gravity (BGM-3 Gravimeter) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw underway gravimeter data (Bell BGM-3, 48 files, ~167 MB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed gravity product (~696 MB) is also available via RVData.

data.name: MGL1610 Magnetics (G-882 Magnetometer) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw magnetometer data (Geometrics G-882, 65 files, ~2.6 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed magnetics product (~2.5 GB) is also available.

data.name: MGL1610 Navigation/Positions (C-Nav 3050) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Primary navigation (GPS positions) data from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise, C-Nav 3050 system, archived via Rolling Deck to Repository (R2R).

data.name: MGL1610 Seismic Navigation — Shot Time (ASCII, Processed) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Processed seismic shot time navigation data from the MGL1610 PICTURES cruise, offshore Chile. For use when processing the MCS reflection data.

data.name: MGL1610 Subbottom Profiler (Knudsen 3260) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw singlebeam/subbottom profiler data (Knudsen 3260, 50 files, ~1.1 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise.

processing.summary: Multichannel seismic reflection data from the MGL1610 PICTURES cruise processed to obtain depth sections of the forearc structure. Refraction data were jointly inverted to produce velocity models. Data reveal a forearc basin with ~2 km sediment fill, consistent with long-term subduction erosion subsidence.
workflow: 6. Joint refraction tomography (OBS + MCS first arrivals)
processing.notes: Four trench-perpendicular profiles between 19°S and 21°S. Sediment thickness up to ~2 km in forearc basin.

analysis_notes: Paper identified as using MGL1610 (PICTURES) cruise data from 2016. Abstract reconstructed from survey snippet and Semantic Scholar metadata. Specific line coordinates are approximate; exact coordinates available in paper. MGL1610 data confirmed as open access at MGDS (cruise DOI 10.7284/907099). All data types directly downloadable from RVData/MGDS.
